displayTitle: Contact or Immersion? Match the Method

beats.narration: Immersion's finale: three ideas. Plate waves in thin sheets, two ways to arrange the sound, and the honest verdict: contact or immersion?

payload.title: IMMERSION FINALE
payload.sub: Three ideas that tie immersion testing together.

beats.narration: In a sheet about a wavelength thick, a precisely tilted beam couples into a Lamb wave that rides the sheet, sweeping panels for disbonds.

payload.heading: Plate waves ride along thin sheets
payload.note: RIDES THE SHEET · SWEEPS PANELS FOR DISBONDS

beats.narration: Pulse echo: one probe reads echoes, and echo time gives depth. Through transmission: two aligned probes. A flaw drops the received signal. No depth.

payload.heading: Echo locates · transmission detects
payload.note: ECHO TIME = DEPTH · SIGNAL DROP = FLAW, NO DEPTH

beats.narration: Contact or immersion? Immersion wins consistency, coverage, and a recorded map. Contact wins portability and access. Neither is better. The job decides.
Here's the rule: match the method to the job. Part, place, volume, and the flaws you expect. Then the choice makes itself.

payload.sub: Part, place, volume, flaws — then choose.
